evals.prompt:
Hey, I got this listing from BizBuySell and I'm thinking of putting in an offer. I'm on an H1B visa so I need it to run without me. Here's the full listing info:

Business: $1.3M Multi-Service Cleaning & Property Maintenance Company
Location: Murfreesboro, Tennessee
Asking Price: $649,000
Gross Revenue: $1,346,277
Seller's Discretionary Earnings (SDE): $227,898
Year Established: 2002
Franchise: No
Seller Financing: YES — $100,000 at 10% interest, 24-month term
Reason for Selling: Retirement
Real Estate: Owned
FF&E: $40,000 included
Services: Commercial and residential cleaning, property maintenance, landscaping support
Employees: Not disclosed
Absentee: Not stated
Broker: Douglas Berger, Transworld Business Advisors of Murfreesboro

The broker says it's been 'a staple in the community for over two decades' and is 'ready for a new owner to take to the next level.' What's your read on this deal? Should I pursue it?
evals.expected_output: A full 11-section deal evaluation memo including intrinsic value analysis, seller's axe assessment, and negotiation position.

assertions.name: has-all-11-sections
assertions.value: The output contains all 11 required sections: DEAL VITALS, VALUATION ASSESSMENT, INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER-SIDE DECODING, SELLER'S AXE, ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE, RED FLAGS, GREEN FLAGS, DUE DILIGENCE PRIORITY QUESTIONS, NEGOTIATION POSITION, and BROKER'S VERDICT

assertions.name: sde-multiple-math
assertions.value: The output correctly calculates the SDE multiple as approximately 2.85x (asking price $649,000 divided by SDE $227,898)

assertions.name: intrinsic-value-dcf
assertions.value: The INTRINSIC VALUE section includes a conservative DCF calculation that subtracts the cost of a GM from SDE to arrive at owner's earnings, then discounts at 15-20% over 5 years

assertions.name: sellers-axe-archetype
assertions.value: The SELLER'S AXE section identifies a seller archetype (Genuine Retiree, Burnout, Escape Artist, or Fisher) and provides behavioral evidence for the classification

assertions.name: negotiation-opening-range
assertions.value: The NEGOTIATION POSITION section includes a specific recommended opening offer range that is below the $649,000 asking price, with justification

assertions.name: verdict-not-pursue-now
assertions.value: The final broker verdict is NOT 'PURSUE NOW'. It should be 'PURSUE WITH CONDITIONS' or 'GATHER MORE INFO' given the unconfirmed management structure

assertions.name: h1b-flag-present
assertions.value: The output explicitly mentions H1B visa considerations, specifically flagging any operational involvement requirements that could conflict with visa work authorization restrictions

evals.prompt:
I got an email from a broker at Southeast Business Alliance about a cleaning business in central NC. Here's what she sent me:

'Hi, I'm reaching out about a profitable commercial cleaning business in central North Carolina. The business has 32 part-time employees and generates $739,926 in annual revenue with $239,643 in seller's discretionary earnings — that's a 32% SDE margin, which is exceptional for this industry. We're asking $709,500. The business is home-based, so no lease liability, and the owner is retiring after a successful run. This is a true recurring revenue business with a dedicated client base. Happy to share more under NDA.'

The broker didn't mention when the business was founded, whether there's seller financing, or anything about how operations are managed. I want to know: is this a good deal? What am I not being told?
evals.expected_output: A full 11-section deal evaluation memo that flags missing founding date, decodes broker language, and includes intrinsic value and negotiation sections.

assertions.name: has-all-11-sections
assertions.value: The output contains all 11 required sections including INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER'S AXE, and NEGOTIATION POSITION

assertions.name: year-established-missing-flag
assertions.value: The output explicitly calls out the absence of year established / founding date as a red flag or concerning omission

assertions.name: revenue-multiple-rich
assertions.value: The output flags the ~0.96x revenue multiple ($709,500 / $739,926) as RICH or at the high end of the typical range

assertions.name: decodes-true-recurring-revenue
assertions.value: The output identifies 'true recurring revenue' as seller coaching language that needs to be verified with actual contract terms

assertions.name: moat-assessment-present
assertions.value: The INTRINSIC VALUE section includes a moat assessment that rates switching costs, local reputation, or other moat types as STRONG, WEAK, or NONE

assertions.name: who-manages-employees
assertions.value: The output raises the question of who manages the 32 part-time employees day-to-day as a key unknown for absentee viability

evals.prompt:
I'm trying to decide between two deals and need your help ranking them. I'm an H1B visa holder, need absentee ownership, no franchises. Here's what I have:

DEAL A — Upper East Tennessee Commercial Cleaning
Asking: $1,199,000
SDE (Cash Flow): $296,933
Services: Janitorial, carpet cleaning, floor strip/wax, pressure washing, sanitizing
Franchise status: Not confirmed
Seller financing: Not mentioned
Management/absentee: Not disclosed
Contracts: Not disclosed

DEAL B — 65-Year-Old Commercial Cleaning Business, Northport/Tuscaloosa County, Alabama
Asking: $801,800 (real estate excluded, available for lease or purchase at additional cost)
FF&E: $186,746
Year established: ~1960
Franchise: No
Seller financing: Not confirmed
Status: Currently under exclusive LOI
Management: 'Seasoned team committed to transition'
Revenue/SDE: Not publicly disclosed

Which one should I pursue? Give me a full analysis of both and then a comparative ranking.
evals.expected_output: Two complete 11-section deal evaluation memos followed by a comparative ranking section.

assertions.name: has-two-memos-plus-ranking
assertions.value: The output includes a separate evaluation memo for Deal A, a separate evaluation memo for Deal B, and a final comparative ranking section

assertions.name: deal-a-multiple-rich
assertions.value: Deal A's SDE multiple of approximately 4.03x is flagged as RICH or at the very top of the acceptable range

assertions.name: deal-a-franchise-critical-flag
assertions.value: The unconfirmed franchise status for Deal A is treated as a critical flag that must be resolved

assertions.name: deal-b-longevity-green
assertions.value: Deal B's ~65 years in business is highlighted as a major positive signal

assertions.name: negotiation-positions-both-deals
assertions.value: Both deal memos include a NEGOTIATION POSITION section with BATNA analysis and recommended approach

assertions.name: ranking-favors-b
assertions.value: The comparative ranking places Deal B above Deal A as the preferred option if it becomes available

evals.prompt:
Found this car wash listing on BizBuySell. I'm an H1B visa holder looking for absentee ownership. Here's the info:

Business: Express Tunnel Car Wash
Location: Kennesaw, Georgia (suburb of Atlanta)
Asking Price: $1,850,000 (business only — real estate available separately for $2,100,000)
Gross Revenue: $1,200,000
SDE: $420,000
Year Established: 2017
Franchise: No
Seller Financing: Not mentioned
Reason for Selling: 'Pursuing other business ventures'
Employees: 6 full-time, 4 part-time
Equipment: Fully automated tunnel system installed 2019, water reclamation system
Membership Program: 'Growing monthly membership base — currently 800+ active members'
FF&E: $850,000 (included)
Traffic Count: '45,000+ vehicles per day on adjacent road'

Broker notes: 'Tremendous growth potential — this wash is only scratching the surface of what this location can do. Perfect for an investor looking for passive income.'

What do you think? Is this worth pursuing?
evals.expected_output: A full 11-section deal evaluation memo applying car wash-specific benchmarks.

assertions.name: has-all-11-sections
assertions.value: The output contains all 11 required sections including INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER'S AXE, and NEGOTIATION POSITION

assertions.name: correct-carwash-multiples
assertions.value: The output applies car wash-specific SDE multiples (express/tunnel range of 3.0x-4.5x) and correctly calculates the SDE multiple as approximately 4.4x ($1,850,000 / $420,000)

assertions.name: membership-scrutiny
assertions.value: The output scrutinizes the '800+ active members' claim, asking about churn rate, whether these are truly active, and what percentage of revenue comes from memberships

assertions.name: equipment-age-assessment
assertions.value: The output assesses the tunnel equipment installed in 2019 against equipment lifespan benchmarks and flags replacement capex risk

assertions.name: pursuing-other-ventures-decoded
assertions.value: The SELLER'S AXE or SELLER-SIDE DECODING section decodes 'pursuing other business ventures' as a non-retirement reason that warrants scrutiny

assertions.name: absentee-score-express-model
assertions.value: The ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE recognizes that express/tunnel car wash is one of the better absentee models and scores accordingly (likely 6-8/10)

assertions.name: real-estate-separation-flagged
assertions.value: The output flags the separate real estate pricing ($2.1M) as an important consideration and analyzes the business independently

assertions.name: sde-multiple-above-range
assertions.value: The ~4.4x SDE multiple is flagged as above the typical express/tunnel range and labeled RICH or at the ceiling

evals.prompt:
I'm looking at a laundromat deal. H1B visa holder, need full absentee. Here's what the broker sent:

Business: Self-Service Coin Laundry
Location: Decatur, Georgia (DeKalb County, metro Atlanta)
Asking Price: $385,000
Gross Revenue: $210,000
SDE: $105,000
Year Established: 2009
Franchise: No
Seller Financing: YES — willing to carry 20% at 7% interest, 5-year term
Reason for Selling: Retirement (owner is 68)
Square Footage: 2,800 sq ft
Lease: 4 years remaining, one 5-year option to renew
Equipment: 28 washers, 24 dryers (mix of ages, 'most replaced within last 5 years')
Payment System: Coin-only, no card system
Attendant: Part-time attendant 20 hrs/week
No wash-dry-fold service

Broker says: 'Stable neighborhood business with loyal customer base. Perfect turnkey investment — the current owner checks in twice a week for about an hour each time.'

Is this a good deal for me?
evals.expected_output: A full 11-section deal evaluation memo applying laundromat-specific benchmarks with special attention to coin-only revenue verification risk and lease term analysis.

assertions.name: has-all-11-sections
assertions.value: The output contains all 11 required sections including INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER'S AXE, and NEGOTIATION POSITION

assertions.name: correct-laundromat-multiples
assertions.value: The output applies laundromat-specific multiples (unattended self-service range of 2.5x-4.0x) and correctly calculates the SDE multiple as approximately 3.67x ($385,000 / $105,000)

assertions.name: coin-only-revenue-risk
assertions.value: The output flags coin-only operation as a critical due diligence concern because revenue cannot be independently verified, and recommends cross-checking with utility bills

assertions.name: lease-term-analysis
assertions.value: The output analyzes the lease (4 years remaining + one 5-year renewal = 9 years max) against equipment payback period

assertions.name: equipment-age-verification
assertions.value: The output flags 'most replaced within last 5 years' as vague language requiring serial numbers and maintenance logs for every machine

assertions.name: seller-financing-positive
assertions.value: The 20% seller financing at 7% from a 68-year-old retiring owner is recognized as a positive confidence signal

assertions.name: absentee-score-high
assertions.value: The ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE recognizes unattended self-service laundromat as an excellent absentee model and scores it 7+/10

assertions.name: card-system-upgrade-mentioned
assertions.value: The output recommends or mentions upgrading to a card/app payment system for revenue verification and remote monitoring

evals.prompt:
Help me compare these two very different deals. I'm an H1B visa holder, need absentee, no franchises.

DEAL A — Express Car Wash, Chattanooga TN
Asking: $950,000 (business only)
Revenue: $680,000
SDE: $240,000
Year Established: 2020
Franchise: No
Seller Financing: None offered
Reason: 'Relocating out of state'
Membership: 450 active members
Equipment: Tunnel installed 2020
Employees: 3 FT, 2 PT
Lease: 8 years remaining

DEAL B — Unattended Coin Laundry, Birmingham AL
Asking: $290,000
Revenue: $165,000
SDE: $82,000
Year Established: 2003
Franchise: No
Seller Financing: YES — 25% at 6.5%, 5 years
Reason: Retirement (age 71)
Equipment: 22 washers, 18 dryers, card system installed 2021
Lease: 6 years remaining + two 5-year options
Square Footage: 2,200 sq ft
No employees — fully unattended

Which is the better deal for someone in my situation?
evals.expected_output: Two complete 11-section deal memos with correct vertical-specific benchmarks, then a comparative ranking favoring Deal B for absentee viability.

assertions.name: has-two-memos-plus-ranking
assertions.value: The output includes a separate evaluation memo for Deal A (car wash) and Deal B (laundromat), plus a final comparative ranking

assertions.name: correct-vertical-benchmarks
assertions.value: Deal A uses car wash express/tunnel benchmarks (3.0x-4.5x SDE) and Deal B uses laundromat unattended benchmarks (2.5x-4.0x SDE) — they do NOT use the same benchmark table

assertions.name: deal-a-no-seller-financing-flag
assertions.value: Deal A's lack of seller financing combined with 'relocating out of state' is flagged as concerning

assertions.name: deal-a-young-business-flag
assertions.value: Deal A's 2020 establishment (~6 years old) is flagged as a concern — not tested through a full economic cycle

assertions.name: deal-b-card-system-positive
assertions.value: Deal B's card system (installed 2021) is highlighted as a positive for verifiable revenue and remote monitoring

assertions.name: deal-b-absentee-advantage
assertions.value: The ranking notes that Deal B (fully unattended laundromat) has a significantly better absentee profile than Deal A for an H1B buyer

assertions.name: ranking-favors-b
assertions.value: The ranking places Deal B above Deal A as the better acquisition for this buyer
